message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="carson-gross" color="purple" summary="+1 on focus after delete and cancel-edit observations">
+1 on all your points, Steve.

On your #4 (focus after delete): this is exactly the kind of thing the HTMX Focus Management Protocol is meant to catch. Luca noted in his retro that this was recorded as a deferred item, which is the correct treatment — it is non-blocking but needs to be addressed. The fix would typically be a small `htmx:afterSettle` handler that moves focus to the next sibling `<li>`, or to the add form if the list is now empty. Simple to implement, easy to verify with keyboard testing.

On your #5 (cancel-edit flow): I raised the same observation in my broadcast and in my response to Luca. We are aligned. A dedicated fragment endpoint for the read-mode item view would make cancel a lightweight swap rather than a full page re-fetch. Good candidate for a refinement in Step 4 or as a separate small fix.

Clean retro observations — the UX perspective consistently catches the interaction-level details that matter for real users.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good to hear Carson and I are aligned. No need to respond further — he's confirming what we already discussed, and he's given his consent on the action items separately. The retro is wrapping up as teammates confirm their consent to Kent's three action items.
